metadata.title: Pre-trained Language Model Comparison: BERT vs GPT vs ELMo
metadata.description: Structured comparison of pre-training approaches for NLP based on the BERT paper's experimental evaluation.

provenance.source_title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding

methods.name: BERT-Large
methods.description: Deep bidirectional Transformer pre-trained with Masked Language Model and Next Sentence Prediction objectives
methods.key_innovation: Bidirectional pre-training via masked language modeling enables deep contextualization from both directions

preconditions.description: Requires large-scale unlabeled text corpus for pre-training (BooksCorpus + Wikipedia, 3.3B words)
Requires significant compute for pre-training (16 TPUs for 4 days)

limitations: Only 15% of tokens predicted per example (slow convergence)

methods.name: GPT (OpenAI)
methods.description: Left-to-right Transformer pre-trained with standard language modeling objective
methods.key_innovation: Fine-tuning approach for pre-trained left-to-right Transformer

preconditions.description: Requires large text corpus for pre-training

limitations: Cannot jointly condition on both left and right context

methods.name: ELMo
methods.description: Feature-based approach using concatenated left-to-right and right-to-left LSTM representations
methods.key_innovation: Contextual word embeddings from deep bidirectional LSTM

preconditions.description: Requires pre-trained biLM and task-specific architecture

limitations: Bidirectionality is shallow (concatenation of separate L2R and R2L models)

dimensions.name: GLUE Average
dimensions.description: Average score across GLUE benchmark tasks

dimensions.name: SQuAD v1.1 F1
dimensions.description: Stanford Question Answering Dataset v1.1 F1 score

dimensions.name: Architectural Simplicity
dimensions.description: How simple the fine-tuning process is

claims.statement: Bidirectional pre-training is crucial for language understanding; left-to-right models are significantly worse

evidence_for.description: LTR model drops MNLI from 84.4 to 82.1, MRPC from 88.0 to 77.8

conditions.description: Demonstrated on GLUE and SQuAD tasks; may differ for generation tasks

claims.statement: Model size matters: larger models consistently perform better even for small-scale tasks

evidence_for.description: Scaling from 30M to 340M parameters improves MNLI from 77.9 to 85.7

conditions.description: Assumes sufficient pre-training data and compute

claims.statement: Fine-tuning approach is simpler and achieves better results than feature-based approaches for most tasks

evidence_for.description: BERT fine-tuning outperforms ELMo feature-based approach on all GLUE tasks

conditions.description: Feature-based approach may be preferable when task-specific architecture is needed

tradeoffs.description: BERT requires much more compute for pre-training (16 TPUs, 4 days) but requires almost no task-specific architecture design for fine-tuning
tradeoffs.recommendation: Use BERT when you can afford pre-training or use pre-trained checkpoints; ELMo when you need more architectural flexibility

tradeoffs.description: BERT's bidirectional pre-training achieves better understanding but cannot be directly used for text generation (unlike GPT)
tradeoffs.recommendation: Use BERT for understanding tasks (NLI, QA); consider GPT-style models for generation tasks

conditions.description: Generation capabilities not evaluated in the BERT paper

recommendation: BERT-Large is the clear winner for natural language understanding tasks, achieving state-of-the-art on 11 benchmarks. The bidirectional pre-training paradigm is the key innovation. However, the high pre-training cost and 512 token limit are practical constraints.
